growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: corkrey.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Chung, Daehwan et al. Expression of a heat-stable NADPH-dependent alcohol dehydrogenase in Caldicellulosiruptor bescii results in furan aldehyde detoxification. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2015). DOI:10.1186/s13068-015-0287-y. [PMC4511240]
growth_records.snippet: The calculated doubling time of JWCB018 and JWCB044 was ~3.3 h, while that of the wild type was slightly faster at ~3.0 h.
growth_records.notes: Wild-type doubling time ~3.0 h (reported). mu = ln2/3.0 = 0.231 /h (calculated). LOD medium full composition not listed in text (cited ref 23); carbon source cellobiose/maltose 0.5% w/v (5 g/L). No product rates for this inhibitor-free growth condition.

growth_records.cite: Chung, Daehwan et al. Expression of a heat-stable NADPH-dependent alcohol dehydrogenase in Caldicellulosiruptor bescii results in furan aldehyde detoxification. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2015). DOI:10.1186/s13068-015-0287-y. [PMC4511240]
growth_records.snippet: The calculated doubling time of JWCB018 and JWCB044 was ~3.3 h
growth_records.notes: Doubling time ~3.3 h (reported). mu = ln2/3.3 = 0.210 /h (calculated). Lactate-negative (ldh) mutant grows slightly slower than WT; produces more acetate. No product rates for inhibitor-free growth.

growth_records.medium: LOD medium + 5 g/L cellobiose + 15 mM furfural

sec.method: estimated as sum of acetate + biomass

sec.units: mM (±0.8; assumes 100% reduction of removed furfural)

growth_records.cite: Chung, Daehwan et al. Expression of a heat-stable NADPH-dependent alcohol dehydrogenase in Caldicellulosiruptor bescii results in furan aldehyde detoxification. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2015). DOI:10.1186/s13068-015-0287-y. [PMC4511240]
growth_records.snippet: Mass balance of 15 mM furfural or 15 mM 5-HMF containing C. bescii batch fermentations ... JWCB001 [furfural]: Cellobiose consumed 6.5±0.6, Glucose 7.2±0.6, Acetate 7.3±0.6, Lactate 3.4±0.3, CO2 7.7±0.5, Biomass 0.4±0.0, H2 12.9±0.9, Furfur
growth_records.notes: Table 2, 72-h batch endpoint concentrations (mM). Biomass 0.4±0.0 mM C (elemental C4H7O2N; protein=50% CDW). CO2 estimated (acetate+biomass). Average volumetric rates over 72 h: cellobiose 6.5/72=0.090 mM/h, acetate 7.3/72=0.101 mM/h, H2 12.9/72=0.179 mM/h. Biomass-specific rates not derivable (biomass reported in mM C, no gDW time-course).

growth_records.medium: LOD medium + 5 g/L cellobiose + 15 mM furfural

mfa.note: O/R=1.11; carbon recovery 95.40%; lactate ND (ldh knockout)

growth_records.cite: Chung, Daehwan et al. Expression of a heat-stable NADPH-dependent alcohol dehydrogenase in Caldicellulosiruptor bescii results in furan aldehyde detoxification. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2015). DOI:10.1186/s13068-015-0287-y. [PMC4511240]
growth_records.snippet: JWCB018 [furfural]: Cellobiose consumed 5.3±0.3, Glucose 4.6±0.3, Acetate 10.5±0.6, Lactate ND, CO2 10.8±0.8, Biomass 0.4±0.1, H2 15.5±1.2, Furfuryl alcohol 3.2±0.6, Carbon Recovery 95.40%, O/R 1.11
growth_records.notes: Table 2, 72-h endpoint (mM). ldh knockout: no lactate, more acetate (10.5 vs WT 7.3). Avg volumetric rates /72h: cellobiose 0.074, acetate 0.146, H2 0.215 mM/h. Biomass 0.4±0.1 mM C.

growth_records.medium: LOD medium + 5 g/L cellobiose + 15 mM furfural

mfa.note: O/R=0.92; carbon recovery 102.82%; furfural removal 13.1 mM (vs 3.2-3.6 for parent strains)

growth_records.cite: Chung, Daehwan et al. Expression of a heat-stable NADPH-dependent alcohol dehydrogenase in Caldicellulosiruptor bescii results in furan aldehyde detoxification. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2015). DOI:10.1186/s13068-015-0287-y. [PMC4511240]
growth_records.snippet: JWCB044 [furfural]: Cellobiose consumed 6.7±0.4, Glucose 6.0±0.4, Acetate 14.6±1.0, Lactate ND, CO2 15.0±0.7, Biomass 0.5±0.0, H2 18.7±0.7, Furfuryl alcohol 13.1±0.6, Carbon Recovery 102.82%, O/R 0.92
growth_records.notes: Table 2, 72-h endpoint (mM). BdhA-expressing strain removed 13.1 mM furfural (~4x parent). Avg volumetric rates /72h: cellobiose 0.093, acetate 0.203, H2 0.260 mM/h. Furfural declined to 2.1±0.6 mM by 72 h (Fig 4c). Biomass 0.5±0.0 mM C.

mfa.note: O/R=1.03; carbon recovery 100.95%; 5-HMF less toxic than furfural -> higher fluxes

growth_records.cite: Chung, Daehwan et al. Expression of a heat-stable NADPH-dependent alcohol dehydrogenase in Caldicellulosiruptor bescii results in furan aldehyde detoxification. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2015). DOI:10.1186/s13068-015-0287-y. [PMC4511240]
growth_records.snippet: JWCB001 [5-HMF]: Cellobiose consumed 10.1±0.5, Glucose 7.3±0.5, Acetate 14.4±0.5, Lactate 9.8±0.9, CO2 15.6±0.4, Biomass 1.2±0.1, H2 24.5±2.5, 5-HMF alcohol 3.4±0.4, Carbon Recovery 100.95%, O/R 1.03
growth_records.notes: Table 2, 72-h endpoint (mM). Under 5-HMF (less toxic) end-products higher than furfural. Avg volumetric rates /72h: cellobiose 0.140, acetate 0.200, lactate 0.136, H2 0.340 mM/h. Biomass 1.2±0.1 mM C. 5-HMF removed 3.8±0.5 mM (footnote c).

growth_records.cite: Chung, Daehwan et al. Expression of a heat-stable NADPH-dependent alcohol dehydrogenase in Caldicellulosiruptor bescii results in furan aldehyde detoxification. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2015). DOI:10.1186/s13068-015-0287-y. [PMC4511240]
growth_records.snippet: JWCB018 [5-HMF]: Cellobiose consumed 9.5±0.3, Glucose 6.7±0.6, Acetate 17.8±1.2, Lactate ND, CO2 18.8±1.5, Biomass 1.2±0.1, H2 31.9±1.8, 5-HMF alcohol 3.9±0.7, Carbon Recovery 87.39%, O/R 1.01
growth_records.notes: Table 2, 72-h endpoint (mM). ldh knockout: no lactate, acetate elevated to 17.8. Avg volumetric rates /72h: cellobiose 0.132, acetate 0.247, H2 0.443 mM/h. Biomass 1.2±0.1 mM C. 5-HMF removed 4.5±0.7 mM.

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
